messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: The LowMesh™ Outdoor PoE Node is a ruggedized, high-power base station designed to extend and secure your decentralized mesh network. Built for the elements, this device provides a high-performance anchor point for your Meshtastic® or MESHCORE™ network, enabling reliable communication without relying on cellular or internet infrastructure. Perfect for mounting on a roof, mast, or wall, the Outdoor PoE Node is the ultimate solution for extending network range across larger areas, properties, or community networks. It serves as a robust gateway and relay station, ensuring dependable, private messaging and location sharing. Key Features Dual Platform Compatibility: Fully compatible with Meshtastic® and MESHCORE™ software ecosystems. Flash online easily at lowmesh.com. Outdoor-Rated Enclosure: Designed to withstand the elements for permanent outdoor installation. High-Range Hardware: Powered by the high-efficiency RAK4630 module (nRF52840 + SX1262) @ 3.1v for powerful transmission. Power over Ethernet (PoE): Receives both data and power via a single Ethernet cable for simplified installation (cable not included). External N-Type Antenna: Features a standard N-type connector with +3dBi fiberglass antenna. Quick-Flash Updates: Internal USB-C port for easy firmware flashing (USB-C to USB-A cable included). Diagnostic LED: Communication LED for instant status and connection confirmation on the PCB. The Outdoor PoE Node is available at: https://shop.lowmesh.com/products/outdoor-poe-node-v1 (edited)

messages.author: Max
messages.text: These are super neat. Any plans on a 1W version?
messages.content_html: These are super neat. Any plans on a 1W version?

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: Yes, there is a v2 planned that will replace the RAK4630 with a ESP32 and add an eByte 1W module. Besides the benefit of 1W, you will also be able to update/flash the device via local IP address. The v2 development will take a few months to build and ramp up to scale.
messages.content_html: Yes, there is a v2 planned that will replace the RAK4630 with a ESP32 and add an eByte 1W module. Besides the benefit of 1W, you will also be able to update/flash the device via local IP address. The v2 development will take a few months to build and ramp up to scale.
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
Max
These are super neat. Any plans on a 1W version?

messages.author: bluebrolly.
messages.text: I am also thinking about providing a paid monthly management service as an option. Something like 1 or 2 dollars a month to update the device anywhere in the world and get notifications and metrics on your device, down alerts, ect.
